cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the No-Win Scenario
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kirk repeatedly faces unwinnable situations, from command philosophy to combat survival, culminating in Spock's sacrifice to save the ship.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Kirk asserts that commanders must be prepared for no-win situations during a training debrief.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kirk is emotionally shattered after Spock dies to save the Enterprise, embodying the cost of such scenarios.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Kirk has not yet processed how to lead after enduring the ultimate personal loss in service of duty.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reaffirmation of Command and Loyalty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spock repeatedly validates Kirk's command authority, reinforcing their bond and stabilizing Kirk's leadership under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Spock reaffirms Kirk as both superior officer and friend, lending legitimacy to his command.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kirk leads under duress with Spock's analytical support and Saavik's compliance, demonstrating trusted command cohesion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The crew's inexperience raises questions about long-term command reliability despite loyalty.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Strategic Leadership Under Duress
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kirk makes high-stakes tactical decisions against Khan, adapting to power loss, environmental hazards, and time-critical threats.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Enterprise is disabled, facing surrender to Khan with limited power and no weapons.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kirk shifts from offense to survival, ordering emergency warp to escape the Genesis Wave detonation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The ship's ability to achieve warp speed in time to survive remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Isolation and Personal Stagnation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kirk moves from bureaucratic stagnation to frontline command, only to face increasing isolation through loss and mission risk.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Kirk is stuck in an administrative role, emotionally detached and unfulfilled.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Kirk is physically and emotionally isolated, mourning Spock and cut off from support after high-risk decisions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Kirk can reclaim purpose after profound personal and professional loss.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Command Under Crisis: From Simulation to Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Saavik evolves from failing a no-win command simulation to assuming real command under pressure, demonstrating growing confidence and responsibility in high-stakes scenarios.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fails Kobayashi Maru simulation, questions ability to lead in unwinnable situations
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Assumes command of Enterprise for rescue mission, makes independent decisions under risk
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Potential diplomatic conflict from Neutral Zone incursion

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship and Ethical Tension with Kirk
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Saavik develops a mentor-mentee relationship with Kirk, marked by her seeking guidance, challenging his ethics, and gradually earning his trust in operational roles.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Seeks validation from Kirk after test failure, shows vulnerability
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Trusted to command ship and execute critical tasks independently
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tension between her rule-bound approach and Kirk's improvisational ethics

cross_scene_arcs.title: Crisis Response and Tactical Competence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Saavik repeatedly demonstrates composure and effectiveness in emergency situations, from combat response to system management under attack.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Overwhelmed during simulation, orders evacuation after ship disabled
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Performs critical tactical and technical duties under time pressure and combat conditions
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ongoing vulnerability to superior enemy forces

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rule-Bound Thinking vs. Adaptive Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Saavik's strict adherence to protocol and skepticism of unorthodox solutions contrasts with Kirk's improvisational style, creating a recurring tension in her understanding of command.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Believes Kobayashi Maru has a correct solution, seeks procedural answer
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Engages critically with Kirk's reprogramming, maintains rule-bound perspective while questioning leadership ethics
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Conflict between procedural correctness and adaptive leadership in crisis

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship and Development of Saavik
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spock consistently guides Saavik through professional challenges, correcting her biases, reinforcing discipline, and placing her in high-responsibility roles to foster her growth as a Vulcan-Romulan officer in Starfleet.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Saavik expresses prejudice toward humans and lacks confidence in her role
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Spock entrusts Saavik with piloting the Enterprise out of Spacedock, signaling confidence in her development
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Saavik's difficulty with human behavior and social norms remains a challenge

cross_scene_arcs.title: Tactical Partnership with Kirk
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spock and Kirk maintain a deeply synchronized command relationship, combining logic and instinct in high-stakes tactical decisions, from reaffirming loyalty to executing precise combat maneuvers.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Spock reaffirms his loyalty and duty to Kirk as both friend and superior officer
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Spock and Kirk coordinate under pressure to exploit tactical openings, demonstrating mutual trust and strategic alignment
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: potential crisis at Regula I remains unknown

cross_scene_arcs.title: Strategic Decision-Making Under Pressure
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spock identifies and advocates for key tactical strategies, including scanning the Reliant, proposing entry into the Mutara Nebula, and coordinating shield disruption, to counter superior enemy capabilities.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Enterprise faces a faster, better-armed enemy with unclear intent
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Enterprise levels the battlefield by entering the Mutara Nebula, accepting system impairments for tactical parity
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: operational risks due to disabled shields and impaired sensors in the nebula

cross_scene_arcs.title: Self-Sacrifice to Save the Enterprise
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Spock overrides medical protest and enters a lethal radiation environment to repair the warp core, prioritizing the ship's survival over his own, asserting his non-human physiology as both justification and necessity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Warp system failure threatens the Enterprise; repair requires exposure to fatal radiation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Spock enters the reactor room and begins repairs under lethal conditions
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether Spock can survive the radiation exposure
